title: elymus spicatus
intro: Elymus spicatus, also known as red oat grass, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its rhizome and seeds, which are known to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Elymus spicatus include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and soothing digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's rhizome and seeds are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
